summary: Iteration 2 smoke test. Backend endpoints all healthy (quote, shopping CRUD, me-time seed+CRUD, tasks, google/status, oauth/calendar/login 503). Frontend loads with quote banner, stats, bottom nav, and tasks work end-to-end (add via modal-save-btn, share modal with correct hrefs, gcal click handled, reload persistence). CRITICAL: Shopping and Me Time pages crash on add/toggle/remove due to prop-wiring mismatch in App.js (hooks return add/toggle/remove but pages destructure onAdd/onToggle/onRemove). The resulting runtime error pops the webpack-dev-server overlay which blocks any further UI interaction until reload.
passed: GET /api/me-time -> 200 auto-seeded 5 default rituals (Deep breathing, Stretch break, Tea ritual, Walk outside, Mindful pause)
Shopping CRUD via API: POST/PUT/DELETE /api/shopping all return 200
Frontend loads quote-banner with text + author
Bottom nav exposes nav-tasks-btn, nav-shopping-btn, nav-metime-btn, nav-timer-btn, nav-calendar-btn
add-task-btn opens task-form-modal; modal-save-btn creates new task card
share-task-{id} opens share-modal; share-whatsapp/telegram/sms/email-btn have correct external URL hrefs (wa.me, t.me/share/url, sms:, mailto:)
gcal-task-{id} click does not crash (toast shown as expected when not configured)
Task persistence across full page reload (tasks survived reload via backend)

failed.issue: Adding a shopping item triggers 'TypeError: onAdd is not a function' in ShoppingPage submit. Root cause: App.js does `<ShoppingPage {...shopping} />` but useShopping returns {items, add, toggle, remove, clearPurchased} while ShoppingPage destructures {onAdd, onToggle, onRemove, onClearPurchased}.
failed.evidence: Runtime error overlay captured: 'onAdd is not a function at submit (ShoppingPage)'. Fix: map props in App.js e.g. <ShoppingPage items={shopping.items} onAdd={shopping.add} onToggle={shopping.toggle} onRemove={shopping.remove} onClearPurchased={shopping.clearPurchased} />

failed.issue: Same prop-wiring bug: App.js does `<MeTimePage {...meTime} />`. useMeTime returns {items, add, update, remove} but MeTimePage destructures {items, onAdd, onUpdate, onRemove}. metime-add-btn, metime-edit-{id}, metime-remove-{id} will all throw 'onX is not a function'.
failed.evidence: Code inspection: /app/frontend/src/App.js:254 vs /app/frontend/src/pages/MeTimePage.jsx:75 and /app/frontend/src/hooks/useMeTime.js:96. Could not reach the page in UI test because webpack-dev-server-client-overlay (triggered by the Shopping crash) intercepted all subsequent clicks; bug confirmed statically.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/App.js, replace spread props with explicit mapping for ShoppingPage: items={shopping.items} onAdd={shopping.add} onToggle={shopping.toggle} onRemove={shopping.remove} onClearPurchased={shopping.clearPurchased}.
In /app/frontend/src/App.js, do the same for MeTimePage: items={meTime.items} onAdd={meTime.add} onUpdate={meTime.update} onRemove={meTime.remove}.

After fix, retest: shopping add/toggle/remove, metime add/edit/remove, breather overlay (metime-start -> breather-overlay + breather-toggle-btn + breather-close-btn), nav-timer-btn -> timer-widget, nav-calendar-btn -> calendar-not-configured + gcal-connect-btn toast.
Optional: verify localStorage offline persistence for scheduler.shopping.v1 and scheduler.metime.v1 after fix.

success_rate.frontend: ~55% (tasks/share/quote/nav OK; shopping+metime broken; timer/calendar pages not reachable in this run due to overlay block but components themselves appear wired correctly)
